sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 60.8g of fat. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for nuts, fat quality has to justify the calorie density.
sections.subtitleText: 60.8g of fat. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for nuts, fat quality has to justify the calorie density.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 16.7g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for a nut, you need enough upside to make the density worth it.
sections.subtitleText: 16.7g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for a nut, you need enough upside to make the density worth it.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 15g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein is support here, not the whole argument.
sections.subtitleText: 15g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein is support here, not the whole argument.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin e at 100% daily value stands out most. that is major vitamin support.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 41% daily value, zinc at 18% daily value, iron at 26% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: pros first: portable, practical snack food because nuts score real points in life because they are easy to use. fat and texture help satiety because they can hold up better than many snack foods. still a recognisable whole-food option because that keeps them cleaner than most processed snacks.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next: calorie density is very high because that makes portion control a real issue. protein is useful but not elite because nuts help, but they are not protein stars. cost or allergy issues can limit use because practical downsides still count.

closing.summary: Hazelnuts has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best in small amounts where the fats and minerals matter more than sheer calories.
